title: Update compass rose to animate during timeline playback
description: Makes the compass rose Direction Analysis panel update live during timeline playback by computing per-hour sailing condition scores for all 8 directions server-side and rendering them client-side as the timeline advances.

plan.summary:
The `analyzeDirections` function already fetches full hourly weather data for each of the 8 compass directions, but currently only scores the `.current` snapshot. By scoring the hourly data too and including it in the response, the client-side `updateCompassRose` function can re-render the compass per hour as the timeline plays.

This requires changes in 3 places:
1. **analyzeDirections function** — score each hourly entry per direction and add an `hourly` array to each direction result
2. **sailing-weather.js** — update `updateCompassRose` to accept an optional hour index and use hourly scores when available; call it from `advanceTimeline()` and the slider handler
3. **Tests** — update the existing direction validation test to verify the new hourly structure

Tradeoff: the response payload grows from ~8 direction objects to 8 directions × 48 hours of scores. This is still small (~15-20KB) since each hourly entry is just {score, rating, summary} — no raw weather data is sent per hour.

steps.description: Update the `analyzeDirections` function to extract the scoring logic (scoreConditions, getRating, buildSummary) and apply it to each hour in the hourly array for every direction. Each direction result gains an `hourly` array of `{ score, rating, summary }` objects indexed by forecast hour.

Update `sailing-weather.js`: modify `updateCompassRose()` to accept `(directions, hourIndex)`. When hourly data exists and hourIndex > 0, use `dir.hourly[hourIndex]` for score/rating/summary instead of the top-level values. Call `updateCompassRose(directionsData, currentHourIndex)` from `advanceTimeline()`, the slider input handler, and the initial data load.
Update tests: verify the directions API response includes `hourly` arrays on each direction, and verify the hourly arrays have the expected length and structure (score, rating, summary fields).
